samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">Data width.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">FIFO depth.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <para>1. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule.</para>
    <para>2. Use the first four bits of the read/write pointer as the address of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>3. Use the last bit of the read/write pointer as the read/write enable signal of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>4. Use the Gray code to avoid the invalid state between the write and read pointers.</para>
    <para>5. Use the read pointer synchronizer to synchronize the write pointer to the read clock.</para>
    <para>6. Use the write pointer synchronizer to synchronize the read pointer to the write clock.</para>
    <para>7. Use the read pointer synchronizer to synchronize the write pointer to the read clock.</para>
    <para>8. Use the write pointer synchronizer to synchronize the read pointer to the write clock.</para>
    <para>9. Use the synchronized write pointer to determine the full signal.</para>
    <para>10. Use the synchronized read pointer to determine the empty signal.</para>
    </implementation>
</module>
